SC01_HOTEL_LOBBY: Luxury lobby ambient: crystal chandelier faint glass-clink resonance, marble floor reflecting overlapping crowd noise — dozens of simultaneous ripcord rip-pulls layered with spinning-top metal whirr from every stadium in frame, crowd cheering and whooping in waves, occasional whip-crack snap as a launcher releases close to camera — top-on-top collision spark-clack impacts puncturing the wall of noise, one top stuttering and dying with a hollow rattle-stop, the victor's top holding a higher, steadier whirring pitch above the dying one — then floor-penetration: grinding metal-through-marble bore tone rising in pitch, a vortex suction whoosh swallowing the crowd ambient whole, warm noise bleeding into cold near-silence as the descent begins.
SC02_SNOW_FORTRESS: Exposed-ridge wind howling steadily, parka fabric rustle, distant fortress generator drone, snow crunching underfoot as onlookers gather and go still — unlike SC01, near-total hush from the crowd, only wind and breath. Single ripcord rip-pull cutting cleanly through the cold air, spinning-top whirr thin and exposed against the wind bed, one top-on-top clash with a metallic ring that decays slowly into the wind rather than into crowd noise, the losing top stutter-dying into snow with a muffled thud, the winning top's whirr persisting high and lonely — then ice-bore: a cracking, grinding descent tone, vortex pulling snow grain and faint crowd murmur downward, ambient draining from cold white toward grey hush, unresolved.
VO_OVERLAY_SC01_SC02: Protagonist voiceover sits close-mic'd and dry, no room reverb, deliberately interior rather than spoken aloud — ducked just under both the hotel crowd-roar and the snow-wind beds so it reads as thought-track, not dialogue. Tone flat, unhurried, mildly compressed; bleeds continuously across the cut between the two ambiences without re-settling.
SC03_LIMBO_COLLAPSE: Soundless collapse: buildings folding and sliding into the tilted sea with no impact noise, only a sub-threshold structural groan felt more than heard, dust sifting down in near-silence, distant water shifting unnaturally slow. Footsteps on loose debris. A single ripcord pull and top clash open the scene normally — but as the argument escalates, the spinning-top whirr rises and frays in volume alongside the dialogue, the two sound layers visibly tied together; the loser's top disintegrates into a brittle clattering scatter exactly as the argument is lost.
SC03_REALITY_KICK: Hard freeze: every sound stops dead on the same frame — dust, sea, structural groan, all silenced at once. Then reversal: the silence breaks into a rising reverse-suction roar, debris and crowd fragments replayed backward and sped up, a doppler-rising whoosh as everything is pulled upward — brief flash-by audio cues from each layer in reverse (a fragment of chandelier clink, a gust of snow-wind) as he passes back through, accelerating into a single white-noise burst that cuts hard on the match cut.
SC04_OFFICE: Sharp inhale-startle, desk creak as he jerks upright, then a deliberately mundane office hush: sparse keyboard typing, fluorescent ballast hum, a colleague's bag zipper, a chair rolling back, the declining murmur of an emptying floor at end of shift. No emotional scoring, no emphasis — the ordinariness is the point; nothing in the mix tells him, or us, whether it was a dream.
SC04A_MTR_CARRIAGE: MTR carriage interior ambient: steady rail rumble and wheel-on-track clatter through the car, faint door-chime two-tone before closing, recorded announcement clipped and distant under the rumble bed — light passenger murmur, a phone notification chime somewhere down the car, occasional carriage sway creak on the joints. He stands holding the handrail, gaze blank, nothing in the mix signalling dream or memory — same deliberately mundane treatment as SC04, rumble continuing unbroken under the door-open chime as the train pulls into the next station.
SC05_PARK: Quiet night park ambient: distant traffic hush, faint insect chirr, light wind through trees, footsteps slowing to a stop. Garbage bin lid creaks as he notices the discarded stadium; plastic scrape as he lifts it, a soft set-down on the path. One ripcord pull, alone — no crowd, no cheering, the solitude audible by absence compared to SC01. Top whirr spinning unaccompanied, then degrading into an unstable rattling wobble, pitch dropping — cut to black exactly on the rattle, sound severed with the image, no resolution, no final note.
